text.title: ADR AI-Drafted With Review Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the adr-ai-drafted-with-review methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Approver MUST be a named human; '@team' or 'system' is forbidden.
ADR MUST NOT be committed until approver signs off with timestamp.
Context bundle MUST stay within token budget published by the team.
rule.rationale: Bloated bundles waste budget and hide signal.

rule.statement: AI draft MUST follow adr-consequence-evidence-binding rules (anchor + threshold).
Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
